item.title: Trump’s FIFA call was unfair to U.S. soccer fans
item.description: Folarin Balogun's suspension reversal, America 250 celebrations and more, in readers' eyes.

item.title: What Trump’s falling numbers with Hispanics mean for Democrats
item.description: Outreach to the Latino working class should stress economic dignity, not socialist ideology.

item.title: Move over, SAT and ACT. There’s a better test.
item.description: The college entrance exam market has been dominated by two tests eager to lower standards.

item.title: Why the CDC refusing to publish covid vaccine research is so worrying
item.description: The publication of the study in another journal doesn’t erase the troubling questions raised by its suppression.

item.title: Stuck
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Russia-Ukraine war.

item.title: Europe braces for a Russian provocation
NATO 3.0 is rising — and buying American
item.description: The alliance that has leaned too heavily on U.S. muscle is building its own — and buying American, too.

item.title: A 41 percent tax? Hold our beer.
item.description: Check out this interactive to see the fee you pay for your favorite brew.

item.title: Trump plays stock adviser. Think twice before playing along.
item.description: The president’s stock endorsements are legal, but that doesn’t make them good advice.

item.title: John Bolton: To save NATO, smile and thank Trump
item.description: The leaders’ collective top priority for the Ankara summit: Do no harm.

item.title: My fellow Democrats, let this reality about defense tech sink in
item.description: Concerns about how data is used are justified, but they should not be misplaced.

item.title: I’m living a dream at Columbia. Other Afghan girls face a nightmare.
item.description: My brothers started school in March. My sisters had to stay home.
